title: Customer identification program
text: Institutions shall implement a customer identification program that verifies customer identity using reliable, independent source documents, data, or information.

title: Customer due diligence
text: Financial institutions shall understand the nature and purpose of customer relationships to develop a customer risk profile and conduct ongoing monitoring.

title: Enhanced due diligence
text: Enhanced due diligence measures shall be applied to higher-risk customers and transactions, including politically exposed persons and high-risk jurisdictions.

title: Suspicious activity reporting
text: Suspicious transactions shall be promptly investigated and reported to the competent authority when statutory reporting thresholds or indicators are met.

title: Sanctions screening
text: Customers, counterparties, and transactions shall be screened against applicable sanctions lists before onboarding and on an ongoing basis.

title: AML training
text: Relevant personnel shall receive periodic AML/KYC training tailored to their roles, including detection of suspicious behavior and escalation procedures.

title: Retention of KYC records
text: Customer identification and transaction records shall be retained for the minimum legal retention period and be readily retrievable for audit and investigations.

title: Independent AML testing
text: The AML compliance program shall be independently tested on a periodic basis, with deficiencies documented and remediated in a timely manner.
